name: Sourdough Yeast Starter
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the dry yeast in warm water in a mixing bowl. Add flour, stirring until well blended. Cover with plastic wrap and allow to stand at room temperature (or in an oven out of drafts) for at least 48 hours, or until hooch (clear liquid) forms on top.
2. Store in a jar with a loose fitting lid in the refrigerator. Renew monthly by adding alternately 1 cup of water or milk, and 1 cup of flour. Add 1/4 cup of sugar every other month to encourage fermentation.
3. For recipes requiring 3 cups of starter, add 3 cups of water (or milk) and 3 cups of flour. Allow to stand until hooch forms on top. Save 1 cup for future use and use the remaining 3 cups in your recipe.
